rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical transition-metal-free sulfonylative cycloetherification/cycloesterification of unsaturated alcohols (and acids) with sulfonyl hydrazides to give saturated sulfonyl-substituted 5- to 8-membered O-heterocycles
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of sulfonyl hydrazide to its radical cation, which loses H+/N2/H2 to generate the sulfonyl radical; the resulting alkyl radical adduct is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped intramolecularly by the tethered hydroxyl; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered alkyl radical from sulfonyl radical addition to alkene (C)
alkyl carbocation after second anodic oxidation (D)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the sulfonyl hydrazide to a radical cation that loses H+, N2, and H2 to give a sulfonyl radical; this radical adds to the alkene of the unsaturated alcohol to give a benzylic C-radical, which is oxidized again at the anode to a carbocation that is captured by the pendant OH (with loss of H+) to form the cyclic ether; cathode reduces H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: N-phenyl tosylhydrazide (2d-1)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 62% yield, showing the terminal N-H is not essential

probe_reagents.name: N-morpholinyl tosylhydrazide (2d-2)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 54% yield, confirming N-H bonds in hydrazide are non-essential

probe_reagents.name: N-benzoyl-N-morpholinyl tosylhydrazide (2d-3)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 3ad in 35% yield, further supporting non-essential N-H

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3ad not detected; TEMPO-alkyl adduct detected by HRMS, indicating an alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 3ad only in trace; BHT-sulfonyl adduct 7e isolated in 27%, supporting sulfonyl radical

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: 3ad in trace; Ts-CH2-C(Ph)=CHPh adduct detected by GC-MS, supporting sulfonyl radical generation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of 2a at 1.67 V and of 1a at 2.52 V (vs Ag/AgCl) shows the sulfonyl hydrazide is preferentially oxidized at the anode

notes: Alternative pathway where the C-radical C is intercepted by another oxidant rather than oxidized at the anode to a cation cannot be fully excluded, but CV and trapping experiments favor the radical/cation (radical-polar crossover) sequence depicted in Scheme 4. Method is catalyst- and oxidant-free, undivided cell, n-Bu4NClO4 electrolyte, carbon fiber anode / Pt cathode, 10 mA, MeCN, 70 °C.
